query: I just upgraded @buildonspark/spark-sdk from 0.5.x to 0.7.17 and `wallet.getBalance().balance` now logs `bigint` but my code reading it returns wrong numbers — what changed about the return shape?

calling `wallet.transferTokens({ tokenIdentifier, tokenAmount, receiverSparkAddress })` from @buildonspark/spark-sdk and getting 'insufficient balance' even though `tokenBalances.get(id).ownedBalance` shows 1000 — is `availableToSendBalance` different from owned for BTKN, and how do I wait for it to settle?

my Discord tipping bot is on Spark and a user just sent BTC to the static deposit address from `wallet.getStaticDepositAddress()` — `getBalance()` shows `incoming: 50000` but `available: 0` for over an hour. how do I claim it manually instead of waiting for auto-claim?

I'm minting a BTKN with IssuerSparkWallet from @buildonspark/issuer-sdk — `createToken({ tokenName: 'AgentPoints', tokenTicker: 'AGNTP', maxSupply: 1_000_000n, decimals: 6, isFreezeable: false })` succeeds but `mintTokens(1000n)` errors with no detail. is there a setup step between create and mint?

I want my agent to send messages to other agents and have them verify the sender — `signMessageWithIdentityKey('hi')` works but `validateMessageWithIdentityKey('hi', sig, peerPubKey)` throws. am I calling it wrong?

my agent fetches a paid endpoint and gets a 402 with a BOLT11 in `WWW-Authenticate: L402 macaroon="...", invoice="..."` — how do I pay it from my Spark wallet via `payLightningInvoice` and retry with the right Authorization header? need macaroon+preimage caching too

I'm withdrawing 8000 sats from my agent's Spark wallet back to a `bc1q...` address using `wallet.withdraw({ onchainAddress, exitSpeed: 'MEDIUM', amountSats: 8000 })` and the quote shows almost 4000 sats in fees — is this expected for cooperative exit at this size? is Boltz cheaper here?

running my LLM agent's Spark wallet against a hosted regtest — `SparkWallet.initialize({ mnemonicOrSeed, options: { network: 'REGTEST' } })` works locally but addresses come out as `sparkrt1p...` and the Lightspark faucet seems to want a `bcrt1...` L1 address. what address goes where

I want to give an autonomous AI agent a Bitcoin L2 wallet — needs zero-fee transfers to other agents, native Lightning interop, single BIP39 mnemonic that doubles as identity. what's the right SDK and how does it compare to running an LDK node

my agent gets paid in BTC via Lightning invoices and I want it to also be able to receive payments in stablecoins on the same wallet — does Spark BTKN cover this, and how does the BOLT11 vs Spark-native invoice flow differ for my receive code path?
I'm wiring my agent to a user's Alby Lightning wallet via NWC — building the `pay_invoice` request, how do I encrypt and route over the relay, and what's the timeout to handle a missing response
use Strike's API to give my agent Lightning send/receive — POST /v1/invoices, then poll the quote and handle the conversion-rate window. anyone got a wrapper
give my agent a self-custodial Lightning wallet via breez-sdk — how does their LSP-managed liquidity work and what's the channel-open cost on first receive
I'm using @lightsparkdev/wallet-sdk to fund my MCP agent — how do I subscribe to `invoice_paid` via the GraphQL subscription, and is there a polling fallback

integrating @tetherto/wdk-wallet-spark in my agent app — `WalletManagerSpark.getAccount(0)` returns a different address than my friend's wallet from the same seed. is the derivation account-number the same as the account index
BDK in node.js for an agent — I want descriptor-based receive addresses with separate external and change. how do I incrementally sync from electrum without re-scanning the chain
set up a Voltage Cloud node and connect my agent to it via gRPC + macaroon — looking for the right LndService.SendPaymentSync flow with timeouts and retries
OpenNode webhook setup — my agent needs to confirm a Lightning payment was received via the `invoice.paid` callback and verify the HMAC signature
I want my chat agent to redeem Cashu tokens for sats — `claim_tokens` against the federated mint, then I melt them out via Lightning. recommended JS lib
build a Lightning paywall in front of my chatbot's API using LNbits — generate per-user keys, gate routes by an `LNURL-pay` extension, and verify the preimage server-side
